name: Tempered Glass Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Tempered Glass. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Tempered Glass cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Optimal wavelength for Tempered Glass processing with good absorption

spotSize.description: Optimal spot size for precision cleaning on Tempered Glass surface

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Optimal fluence threshold for contaminant removal without glass damage

pulseWidth.description: Optimal pulse width for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Optimal number of passes for thorough cleaning

overlapRatio.description: Optimal overlap ratio for complete coverage without excessive energy deposition

dwellTime.description: Optimal dwell time per cleaning spot

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm fiber lasers in delicate substrate cleaning; starting at 100 W avoids thermal stress on tempered glass (high reflectivity ~8% at 1064 nm, low thermal conductivity ~1 W/m·K); max limited to prevent cracking (melting point ~1400°C). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and study in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on glass surface cleaning.

scan_speed.description: Slower speeds for heavy contamination (e.g., oils/residues) ensure complete removal; 1000 mm/s optimal for light contaminants on tempered glass to balance efficiency and minimize heat accumulation. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and Journal of Cleaning Technology (2020) case studies.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred over picosecond/femtosecond for cost-effective cleaning with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm); 10 ns recommended for tempered glass to selectively ablate contaminants without inducing subsurface stress. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on pulsed laser interactions with glass.

repetition_rate.description: 30 kHz balances cleaning speed with reduced heat buildup (high rates increase accumulation, risking tempered glass fracture); typical for 1064 nm cleaning of transparent substrates. Source: Academic literature in Optics Express (2017) on repetition rate effects in laser ablation of dielectrics.

energy_density.description: Fluence below tempered glass damage threshold (~4-6 J/cm² at 1064 nm) for safe contaminant removal; 1.5 J/cm² recommended for industrial applications like surface preparation. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2021) and SPIE proceedings on laser cleaning of optics.

spot_size.description: 200 μm spot for uniform coverage on large areas of tempered glass; smaller for fine details, larger for broad cleaning to reduce passes. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaning systems.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass (2 recommended) for complete removal of layered contaminants without exceeding thermal limits; single pass sufficient for light soils on tempered glass. Source: Industry practice in Laser Cleaning Handbook (2022) and case studies from glass manufacturing (e.g., Pilkington reports).

overlap_ratio.description: 50% overlap ensures uniform cleaning without gaps or overexposure on reflective tempered glass surfaces; higher for heavy contamination. Source: Process optimization studies in Surface and Coatings Technology (2016).
